sourcesContent:
# Schema DSL: the validation kinds through every corpus layer.
# The byte layer pins the emission without runtime bodies, so the
# corpus body stays runtime-free — the eval tiers live in
# the schema test file against the runtime module.

schema.defaultMaxString = 80

SignupInput = schema
  email!    email
  password! string, 8..100, /[A-Z]/
  handle?   string, 2..24
  invite?   string, [null]

Address = schema :shape
  street! string
  city!   string
  zip!    zip
  full: ~> @street + ", " + @city
  label: (prefix) -> prefix + ": " + @full

Timestamps = schema :mixin
  createdAt! datetime
  updatedAt! datetime

User = schema :shape
  name!  string
  email! email
  bio?   text
  tags?  string[]
  addr?  Address
  @mixin Timestamps

Role = schema :enum
  :admin
  :editor
  :viewer

Status = schema :enum
  :pending 0
  :active  1
  :done    2

Click = schema :shape
  kind! "click"
  x! integer
  y! integer

Scroll = schema :shape
  kind! "scroll"
  dy! integer

Event = schema :union
  @on :kind
  Click
  Scroll

# `when` is a keyword-named field (a legitimate column name); the `?`
# modifier after a keyword word rejects at scan (deliberately strict,
# pinned in test/schema.test.js) so the corpus spells it required.
Wire = schema
  count!  ~integer
  active? ~boolean, [true]
  when!   date

Form = schema :input
  pw!  string, 8..
  pw2! string
  @ensure "passwords must match", :pw2, (u) -> u.pw is u.pw2

Report = schema :shape
  first! string
  last!  string
  full!  string, -> it.first + " " + it.last
  size:  !> @first.length + @last.length
  shout: ->
    s = @full
    s.toUpperCase()

Compact = schema :shape; a!; b? integer; c? "x" | "y"

View = User.omit("bio")
